title: payment_watcher: Sapling payment address logged at info level links subscriber identity to address
description: relay/src/payment_watcher.rs lines 307, 319, 330: the reconstructed Sapling bech32 payment address (address_str / address_str) is logged at tracing::info level alongside the subscriber's pub_id. DESIGN.md line 332 explicitly permits logging addresses ('Log only addresses, tx hashes...'), but logging the address alongside pub_id creates a linkability record in the relay operator's logs: address X belongs to subscriber Y. For a privacy coin payment system, this correlation is the very linkage that shielded addresses are designed to prevent. The address is merchant-generated and already known to the relay as the invoice address, but logging it at info (not debug) makes it appear in production log aggregators. Severity: operational privacy concern — relay operator logs reveal the address-to-identity mapping.
